title: Calliopea aurea

taxonomy: Calliopea aurea is a species of sea hares, a type of marine slugs that belong to the phylum Mollusca and class Gastropoda. They are characterized by their bright yellow color and are found in the waters of the Mediterranean Sea. Calliopea aurea is specifically classified as a type of nudibranch, a group of slimy, soft-bodied marine animals. Within the class Gastropoda, they are part of the suborder Opisthobranchia, which includes other types of marine slugs and sea hares. The classification of Calliopea aurea is further detailed at the family, genus, and species levels as Elysia viridis is not the correct classification however the correct classification is as the following: Calliopea aurea is classified as a member of the family Aplysiidae, the genus Calliopea, and the species C. aurea.
